title: Shots of VTOL taking off or landing
display_title: Shots of VTOL taking off or landing
wikitext:
[]: Shots of VTOL taking off or landing?             Quote Post by someguy » Wed May 02, 2012 9:22 pm Hi, I'm putting together a little video, and for part of it I need a shot or a few shots of a VTOL taking off or landing (what I need more precisely is taking off but I can reverse a shot of one landing). Right now I've got the shot of the VTOL landing at that graveyard where Shinji and Gendo visit his mother's grave amongst the field of graves, but it didn't fill enough space. I've tried skimming through the episodes to find something but honestly it's a pretty big hassle to skim through 26 episodes on 5 DVDs to find a shot that might only last a few seconds. I thought it might be easier to see if anyone knows one off the top of their head so I could jump straight to it. Any ideas? Thanks for the help. I tried Google but there's a problem searching "take off" and "landing" for a VTOL...

[]:             Quote Post by Drabant » Thu May 03, 2012 2:38 pm You won't ever see a full shot of a EVA VTOL landing, because it can't. It's "legs" are too short. Not kidding here, the two primary exhaust engines get in in way of it landing using the landing gear. I was hoping the Rebuild redesigns would fix that, but no sir. So if you need footage from Evangelion, you're probably out of luck.

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Thu May 03, 2012 2:45 pm Interesting. Quite the detailed observation there :P . It's all right if the shot doesn't show the actual contact with the ground (in the scene I referenced in the graveyard, you can see the VTOL settle, but I don't remember if you can see the legs at the moment. Do the engines rotate though?) Maybe it could land if they rotate. Anyway, the important thing is just that it's a shot where it is pretty close to the ground and moving vertically, anything of one actually taking off or landing, or I could make appear to be taking off or landing, is grand.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Thu May 03, 2012 5:07 pm Drabant wrote: You won't ever see a full shot of a EVA VTOL landing, because it can't. It's "legs" are too short. Not kidding here, the two primary exhaust engines get in in way of it landing using the landing gear. I was hoping the Rebuild redesigns would fix that, but no sir. So if you need footage from Evangelion, you're probably out of luck.

[]:             Quote Post by Drabant » Thu May 03, 2012 6:45 pm someguy wrote: stuff

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Thu May 03, 2012 7:10 pm Drabant wrote: But anyway, they conveniently avoid showing the VTOL (YAGR-3B apparently) touching down, and this cap here illustrates the problem it would have landing: [url]http://i.imgur.com/K7n9W.jpg[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Thu May 03, 2012 7:38 pm For at least part of the Iraq war we were losing more soldiers to helicoptor crashes than to combat IIRC. Drabant, I challenge your screenshot with a higher resolution and quality one: I should have added I am not planning on watching the new movies until they've all been released, which I assume will be the same time Half-Life 2: Episode 3 is released. The rest of the video is from the original series, so that's what I need.

[]:             Quote Post by Drabant » Fri May 04, 2012 10:33 am someguy wrote: For at least part of the Iraq war we were losing more soldiers to helicoptor crashes than to combat IIRC. Drabant, I challenge your screenshot with a higher resolution and quality one: I should have added I am not planning on watching the new movies until they've all been released, which I assume will be the same time Half-Life 2: Episode 3 is released. The rest of the video is from the original series, so that's what I need.

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Fri May 04, 2012 11:31 pm Drabant wrote: Maybe my browser is fucked, but that only reads as 200 times 200 something pixels for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Sun May 06, 2012 10:35 am Drabant wrote: Yes, it's a very dangerous craft to sit in, but I'm more amazed that they managed to land no more than four ospreys on the same airfield without at least three of them crashing and burning. It was a miracle of the universe. To further blow away my sense of disbelief, they had hundreds of them flying at the same time in another scene, without raining down in flames. But anyway, they conveniently avoid showing the VTOL (YAGR-3B apparently) touching down, and this cap here illustrates the problem it would have landing: [url]http://i.imgur.com/K7n9W.jpg[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Drabant » Mon May 07, 2012 7:58 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: Like 'Chutes said, the Ospray's memetic problems are only that: memetic. T

[]:             Quote Post by tmantran » Fri May 11, 2012 8:11 pm Airplane nerd here. The V-22's issues were all fixed (at least the ones after the last grounding). Since it became operational, only 1 or 2 have crashed I think. As for the YAGR-3B, there's simply too much vertical surface area. It'd be very unstable in yaw. Pitch control would probably suck too.

[]:             Quote Post by Drabant » Sat May 12, 2012 1:19 pm tmantran wrote: Airplane nerd here. The V-22's issues were all fixed (at least the ones after the last grounding). Since it became operational, only 1 or 2 have crashed I think. As for the YAGR-3B, there's simply too much vertical surface area. It'd be very unstable in yaw. Pitch control would probably suck too.

[]:             Quote Post by Redaye » Sat May 12, 2012 6:38 pm Drabant wrote: Interesting. How would you redesign it? Do the smart thing and just replace it with a chopper?
